chapter_context.summary: Announces the anointed messenger who proclaims good tidings to the meek, binds up the brokenhearted, and gives the garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness.

outline.text: Anointed Messenger of Glad Tidings; Comfort to Brokenhearted
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Anointed Messenger of Glad Tidings; Comfort to Brokenhearted (61:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Rebuilding Old Wastes and Double Reward of Joy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuilding Old Wastes and Double Reward of Joy (61:4-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Garments of Salvation and Righteousness Springing
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Garments of Salvation and Righteousness Springing (61:10-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Glory and Future Hope
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of glory and future hope, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 61, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
